name: Cloquet Country Club
about: Cloquet Country Club is a private country club featuring an on-site golf simulator facility. The club operates on a membership model and offers amenities such as leagues, events, and lessons. The simulator, which is a key feature of the venue, operates seasonally, typically starting in December. The club also provides a calendar of events and opportunities for member participation in various golf activities. Course details indicate the facility has two distinct nines, with the front nine designed by Donald Ross and the back nine by Joel Goldstrand.
why_golfers_like_it: Access to a premium, seasonally operated golf simulator facility
Membership-based access to club amenities and events
Convenient on-site lessons from PGA professionals
Host for prestigious local tournaments like the Cloquet Invitational

food_and_drink.notes: Country club with kitchen facilities; BYOB policy not explicitly mentioned
Full service bar available for club members and guests

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: The venue does not specify whether club rentals are available, so it is recommended that golfers bring their own clubs for use in the simulator.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drink?
faq.answer: The club has kitchen facilities and a full bar, but BYOB (Bring Your Own Beverage) policies are not explicitly stated on the website.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes on the simulator?
faq.answer: The website does not specify the average time required to play 18 holes on the simulator, but most indoor facilities typically range from 60 to 90 minutes depending on player speed and simulator software.

faq.question: Is the golf simulator kid-friendly?
faq.answer: There is no specific information about junior-oriented features or programs for the simulator, but the club does offer a junior golf program for on-course play.
